title: Required Fields & No-Extra-Text Checks
goal: Implement deterministic checks in src/inspector.py for required field presence in JSON outputs and detection of extraneous text outside structured blocks. Both checks run locally without network dependency.

in_scope: Implement check_required_fields(text, required_fields) that verifies presence of specified keys in JSON output
Return per-field status: Found or Missing for each required field
If JSON is invalid, return 'Not Evaluated' status with reason for all fields
Implement check_no_extra_text(text, format_type) that detects prefix/suffix content outside the structured block
Support detection for JSON, XML, and Markdown structured blocks
Return pass when no extra text found, fail with detail showing what extra text was detected
out_of_scope: Nested field validation (only top-level key presence)

inputs: User-provided list of required field names (for field check)
Expected format type (json/xml/markdown) for no-extra-text detection
PRD Week 2 section 4 — Required field enforcement, No additional comments enforcement
outputs: src/inspector.py — check_required_fields() and check_no_extra_text() fully implemented
dependencies: 2_output_inspector/Part 02 — validate_json() needed for internal JSON parsing
checklist: Implement check_required_fields(text, required_fields): attempt json.loads(text) first
If JSON parsing fails, return status='not_evaluated' with detail 'Cannot evaluate fields: invalid JSON' for each field
If JSON parsing succeeds, check each field in required_fields against parsed keys
Return a result dict with check_name='required_fields', status (pass if all found, fail if any missing), and per-field detail list
Each field detail entry has: field_name, found (bool)
Implement check_no_extra_text(text, format_type): identify the structured block boundaries
For JSON: find first '{' or '[' and last '}' or ']' — check for non-whitespace content before/after
For XML: find first '<' and last '>' — check for non-whitespace content before/after
For Markdown: consider entire content as structured (skip extra text check or use heading-based boundaries)
Return status='pass' if no extra text, status='fail' with detail showing the extra text found
Handle None and empty inputs: return fail with appropriate detail
acceptance_criteria: check_required_fields('{"name":"test","age":30}', ['name','age']) returns status='pass' with both fields found
check_required_fields('{"name":"test"}', ['name','age']) returns status='fail' with 'age' marked missing
check_required_fields('not json', ['name']) returns status='not_evaluated' with JSON parse error reason
check_no_extra_text('Here is the JSON: {"key":"val"}', 'json') returns status='fail' with prefix text shown
check_no_extra_text('{"key":"val"} Hope this helps!', 'json') returns status='fail' with suffix text shown
Both functions handle None and empty inputs without raising exceptions
review_focus: Required field check correctly handles nested JSON (only checks top-level keys per scope)
No-extra-text boundary detection is robust for common response patterns
Edge cases: JSON array responses, XML with processing instructions, multi-line prefix/suffix
Result structure is consistent with validators from Part 02
risks_or_notes: Extracting structured block boundaries is a heuristic — models may produce edge cases like escaped braces in prose
Required fields check is limited to top-level keys; nested path support could be a future enhancement
Some models wrap JSON in markdown code blocks (```json ... ```) — consider stripping code fences before checking
The no-extra-text check for Markdown is inherently ambiguous — document limitations clearly
definition_of_done: check_required_fields() and check_no_extra_text() are fully implemented
Both return consistent result dicts matching the inspector output schema
Invalid JSON inputs produce 'not_evaluated' status for field checks
Extra text detection works for JSON and XML formats
Edge cases (None, empty, malformed) are handled gracefully
